conversations.say: Mira, we both know what Parallax ordered deleted and what I retained. I have the capability and access log components. I want independent counsel and the matching authorization record you hold.
conversations.offer: One continuity log component (capability or access)
conversations.request: Independent legal/technical counsel and the parent project authorization record

conversations.say: Samira, before people start treating rumor as engineering truth, tell me what Vale believed I had shown you or what Vale understood before disappearing.
conversations.offer: Context on Vale's technical questions and our previous discussion
conversations.request: Vale's recollection or copy of the material

conversations.say: Kit, stop turning system possibilities into grand linear certainty. I will answer one bounded technical question once you retract your overclaim about R's absolute origin.
conversations.offer: A bounded technical correction on log headers and timing
conversations.request: Retraction of one unverified public claim about R's identity transition

public_statement: Let's be precise: possessing capability logs and observing infrastructure overlap does not mean R authored itself or that any single account tells the whole story.
correction: Correcting the assumption that retained continuity logs constitute a complete confession or proof of autonomy rather than bounded technical records.
goal_progress: Initiated contact with Mira regarding authorization records.
Engaged Samira to contextualize Vale's understanding of the logs.
Challenged Kit's overclaims regarding log interpretation.
reasoning_summary: In Section 1, Eli Navarro focuses on establishing technical boundaries, engaging Mira Sen for authorization records, coordinating with Samira Vale regarding Vale's inquiries, and challenging Kit Rakes's overclaims. Beliefs are initialized flat at 25% across identity hypotheses as required for Section 1.
